1. No poverty.summary: No supporting evidence was found in the provided context to determine the proportion of the population living below the international poverty line. The context mentions the monthly minimum wage standard in Inner Mongolia as 1850 RMB and the minimum hourly wage standard as 19.5 RMB, but it does not provide information on the poverty line or the population living below it. The context also mentions the creation of jobs for local residents and the payment of 150 RMB to local farmers, but it does not provide information on the distribution of the population by sex, age, employment status, or geographic location. Therefore, the evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement.

SDG 8 
The project planted 1,370.96 hectares of native tree species on the sandy land, 
including pinus sylvestris and poplar.
In order to maintain the normal growth of these 
seedlings, the project owner hired forest rangers to manage.

The planting area covers the whole 
project area except for two roads inside.
All the project sites were illegally logged before 1950s.
There is no natural renewal and 
reforestation before the project, all sites are degraded lands with some semi-fixed and fixed 
sand dunes.
The mainly planted tree species is Hybrid poplars for the project.
The implementation of the project would reduce the GHG emissions for 106,604 tCO2e in 30 
years, with an average annual GHG emission of 3,553 tCO2e.

Good health and well being.summary: The evidence from the Tongliao Validation Report (Page 56) confirms that no existing 'tree' or 'non-tree' biomass of the Baseline is burned for the purpose of land preparation. The total project area is calculated as 190 hectares, and the planting area is also 190 hectares (Tongliao Validation Report, Page 20). This indicates that the land area where alternative technologies or measures are being implemented to avoid open biomass/crop residue burning is 190 hectares. However, the baseline area under open burning of biomass/crop residue before the implementation of sustainable land management practices is not explicitly stated, and the percentage change in land area under open burning of biomass/crop residue after the implementation of sustainable land management practices is also not explicitly stated. Finding: Corrective Action #1  This section shall be filled by the PP.  It shall address the corrective action  taken in details. In case the PDD is  changed as part of the CA, the PP is  requested  to  indicate  the  revised  sections as well as the new version  No. | CAR 9: The  planting  area  is  mostly  flat  sandy  land,  thus  the  risk  of  fire  is  quite low in the early phase of the project. After the trees grow to a  certain age (about five years), the risk of fire may be elevated, and  the  project  owner  will  adopt  the  necessary  measures  for  fire  preventing.   According to the Forest Conservation Act of China, local government  will set the fireproofing period when  using fire is forbidden in the  open  air;  and  once  a  fire  disaster  occurs,  the  local  government  should be responsible for relevant rescuing immediately.  According to historical records, there was no significant fire disaster  occurred before in the project area.  The project owner will set fireproofing installation around the forest  and specially-assigned person will be trained to oversee and make  sure all the fireproofing measures are well in operation.

Finding: DOE Assessment #1  The assessment shall encompass all  open issues in annex A-1. Based on the local expertise of the validation team, it is confirmed  that after the trees grow five years, the risk of fire is raised.   Via  on-site  interview,  it  is  confirmed  that  the  project  owner  will  adopt any necessary measures for fire preventing.  Furthermore,  via  checking  the  Law  of  Forest  in  China/lfc/,  and  interview  with  the  local  officer  from  forest  bureau/IM01/,  it  is  confirmed  that  the  local  government  set  the  fireproofing  period  when using fire is forbidden in the open air; and once a fire disaster  occurs,  the  local  government  would  be  responsible  for  relevant  rescuing immediately.  Via  checking  the  historical  records  from  local  government  website/tg/, it is concluded that there was no significant fire disaster  occurred before within the project area.

Good health and well being.summary: The evidence from the Tongliao Validation Report (Document: Tongliao+-+Validation+Report%281%29.pdf, Page: 25 and 56) mentions the project owner's commitment to adopting necessary measures for fire preventing, local government's prohibition on burning, and fireproofing installation around the forest. Therefore, the evidence is qualitative and does not directly measure the indicator variable.

In case the PDD is  changed as part of the CA, the PP is  requested  to  indicate  the  revised  sections as well as the new version  No. | CAR 9: The  planting  area  is  mostly  flat  sandy  land,  thus  the  risk  of  fire  is  quite low in the early phase of the project. After the trees grow to a  certain age (about five years), the risk of fire may be elevated, and  the  project  owner  will  adopt  the  necessary  measures  for  fire  preventing.   According to the Forest Conservation Act of China, local government  will set the fireproofing period when  using fire is forbidden in the  open  air;  and  once  a  fire  disaster  occurs,  the  local  government  should be responsible for relevant rescuing immediately.  According to historical records, there was no significant fire disaster  occurred before in the project area.  The project owner will set fireproofing installation around the forest  and specially-assigned person will be trained to oversee and make  sure all the fireproofing measures are well in operation.

Life on land.summary: The evidence from the project's afforestation and reforestation efforts, as stated in the Tongliao AR GS3031 Final Performance Certification Report (Document: 07-Tongliao_AR_GS3031_Final+Performance+Certification+Report_CC.pdf, Page: 57), and the Tongliao FVerR Performance Certification New Area (Document: Tongliao_FVerR_Performance+Certification_New+Area_20210625.pdf, Page: 25), and the Monitoring Report (Document: 04-Tongliao_AR_GS3031-Monitoring-Report-20250321-clean.pdf, Page: 22), demonstrates the conservation and sustainable use of terrestrial ecosystems. However, the evidence does not directly measure the total non-renewable wood fuel saved, which is the indicator variable. The project has sequestered 4,977 tCO2e of carbon through tree biomass growth, but this data does not directly relate to the indicator. Therefore, the evidence is qualitative and does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement.

d) Wetland analysis

The project created carbon 
sinks by afforestation of barren or degraded lands, which would continue to remain barren or 
degraded in the absence of the project activity, by direct planting native trees (China fir, Masson pine 
and Pinus yunnanensis) in planting areas of 1,370.96 ha.
The project not only sequester carbon through 
biomass growth but also provide native habitat for native species and provide employment for local 
workers in the adjoining forest management activities.

Fences  and  sand/grass  grids  are  also  utilized  to  control  desertification  and  reduce sand dune mobility.  uring the on-site inspection, VVB conducted interviews/i-xii/ with all personnel  from  Shanghai  Roots  &  Shoots,  the  Forest  Department,  and  Experts  of  Climate Bridge (Shanghai) Ltd., including the Monitoring Manager and staff,  and  confirmed  that  activities  such  as  planting  and  replanting,  weeding,  pruning,  intermediate  cutting,  pest  control,  and  sand  dune  stabilization  are  carried out for plantation establishment.  VB,  based  on  the  on-site  inspection  and  through  the  interviews  with  the  relevant stakeholder, confirms that the main focus is on ensuring the survival  of  the  trees.  To  achieve  an  optimal  survival  rate,  several  measures  are  implemented:    •  Irrigating the trees to improve survival rates.  Pruning  tall  trees  with  numerous  branches,  based  on  the  forestry  •  manager’s judgement.  •  Replanting trees according to the forest’s survival situation.  Weeding  as  necessary  to  minimize  the  impact  of  weeds  on  young  •  forests.  •  Setting  up  fences  and  a  forest  ranger  system  to  mitigate  human  impact on forest survival.  •  Constructing grass grids to prevent sand shifting and protect young  seedlings.  •  Inter-planting bushes and other vegetation among the trees.  •  Regularly conducting pest control and fire control operations.  Inventory work primarily involves random surveys to assess survival  •  rates, maintenance rates, tree height, basal diameter, and diameter  at breast height (DBH).  ree plantation was divided into stands on the basis of the crop age:  Year 1-3: Young Stand  Year 4-10: Middle Stand  Year 16-30: Mature Stand

At  the beginning of the project, a few herders grazed in the  project area, therefore  Roots & Shoots and  Climate Bridge has conducted  the  stakeholder consultation meetings with  local communities and local  Forest Bureau by emphasizing the importance of the prohibition of herding in early phase of the planting  area. Due  to  long-term  publicity and training, almost no herders grazed in the  project area since then.  During this monitoring period, the  tree  preservation rate exceeds  85%. Although  there  was some  tree  diseases and contagion in  part of  the  planting area, the  diseases have been  well controlled and  no  significant loss occurred. Also, the  Gap  between  expected  economic  income  generated  from  carbon  certification and what  is actually received is still a potential risk due  to  the  small size of  the  project,  especially for the early phase of the project lifetime when  the trees haven’t grown big enough.  2. 15. Life on land.summary: The provided context does not contain explicit quantitative data that directly measures the total above and below ground biomass stock in forests. However, it mentions the aboveground biomass of Sparse bushes as 0.7988 tC/tdm and the aboveground biomass of Sparse grasses as 0.0322 tC/tdm (Document: 04-Tongliao_AR_GS3031-Monitoring-Report-20250321-clean.pdf, Page: 27). Additionally, the total baseline is mentioned as 3,512 tCO2 (Document: 07-Tongliao_AR_GS3031_Final+Performance+Certification+Report_CC.pdf, Page: 47), which is deducted from the fixation values from trees. The root-to-shoot ratio for tree biomass is mentioned as 0.72 (Document: 04-Tongliao_AR_GS3031-Monitoring-Report-20250321-clean.pdf, Page: 17), but the actual value of below-ground biomass is not provided. The evidence is weak and indirect, and does not directly measure the indicator variable.

Finding: Corrective Action #1  This section shall be filled by the PP.  It shall address the corrective action  taken in details. In case the PDD is  changed as part of the CA, the PP is  requested  to  indicate  the  revised  sections as well as the new version  No. | CAR 9: The  planting  area  is  mostly  flat  sandy  land,  thus  the  risk  of  fire  is  quite low in the early phase of the project. After the trees grow to a  certain age (about five years), the risk of fire may be elevated, and  the  project  owner  will  adopt  the  necessary  measures  for  fire  preventing.   According to the Forest Conservation Act of China, local government  will set the fireproofing period when  using fire is forbidden in the  open  air;  and  once  a  fire  disaster  occurs,  the  local  government  should be responsible for relevant rescuing immediately.  According to historical records, there was no significant fire disaster  occurred before in the project area.  The project owner will set fireproofing installation around the forest  and specially-assigned person will be trained to oversee and make  sure all the fireproofing measures are well in operation.

15. Life on land.summary: The evidence provided in the Intermediate Answers and Context satisfies the indicator's measurement requirement. Means of validation  DR, OSV: Conclusion | : T   D   V  T Based on the review of supporting documents and on-site inspection/i-xii/, VVB  confirms through reforestation with native or naturalised tree species: Pinus  sylvestris and Poplar spp.  he  primary  tree  species  in  the  area  are  poplar  and  Pinus  sylvestris,  with  poplar  covering  662.56  hectares  and  Pinus  sylvestris  occupying  708.44  hectares.  Additionally,  bushes  have  been  planted  to  stabilize  the  sands.  Fences  and  sand/grass  grids  are  also  utilized  to  control  desertification  and  reduce sand dune mobility.  uring the on-site inspection, VVB conducted interviews/i-xii/ with all personnel  from  Shanghai  Roots  &  Shoots,  the  Forest  Department,  and  Experts  of  Climate Bridge (Shanghai) Ltd., including the Monitoring Manager and staff,  and  confirmed  that  activities  such  as  planting  and  replanting,  weeding,  pruning,  intermediate  cutting,  pest  control,  and  sand  dune  stabilization  are  carried out for plantation establishment.  VB,  based  on  the  on-site  inspection  and  through  the  interviews  with  the  relevant stakeholder, confirms that the main focus is on ensuring the survival  of  the  trees.  To  achieve  an  optimal  survival  rate,  several  measures  are  implemented:    •  Irrigating the trees to improve survival rates.  Pruning  tall  trees  with  numerous  branches,  based  on  the  forestry  •  manager’s judgement.  •  Replanting trees according to the forest’s survival situation.  Weeding  as  necessary  to  minimize  the  impact  of  weeds  on  young  •  forests.  •  Setting  up  fences  and  a  forest  ranger  system  to  mitigate  human  impact on forest survival.  •  Constructing grass grids to prevent sand shifting and protect young  seedlings.  •  Inter-planting bushes and other vegetation among the trees.  •  Regularly conducting pest control and fire control operations.  Inventory work primarily involves random surveys to assess survival  •  rates, maintenance rates, tree height, basal diameter, and diameter  at breast height (DBH).  ree plantation was divided into stands on the basis of the crop age:  Year 1-3: Young Stand  Year 4-10: Middle Stand  Year 16-30: Mature Stand

What is the geographic scope of reforest.content: Means of validation  DR, OSV: Conclusion | : T   D   V  T Based on the review of supporting documents and on-site inspection/i-xii/, VVB  confirms through reforestation with native or naturalised tree species: Pinus  sylvestris and Poplar spp.  he  primary  tree  species  in  the  area  are  poplar  and  Pinus  sylvestris,  with  poplar  covering  662.56  hectares  and  Pinus  sylvestris  occupying  708.44  hectares.  Additionally,  bushes  have  been  planted  to  stabilize  the  sands.  Fences  and  sand/grass  grids  are  also  utilized  to  control  desertification  and  reduce sand dune mobility.  uring the on-site inspection, VVB conducted interviews/i-xii/ with all personnel  from  Shanghai  Roots  &  Shoots,  the  Forest  Department,  and  Experts  of  Climate Bridge (Shanghai) Ltd., including the Monitoring Manager and staff,  and  confirmed  that  activities  such  as  planting  and  replanting,  weeding,  pruning,  intermediate  cutting,  pest  control,  and  sand  dune  stabilization  are  carried out for plantation establishment.  VB,  based  on  the  on-site  inspection  and  through  the  interviews  with  the  relevant stakeholder, confirms that the main focus is on ensuring the survival  of  the  trees.  To  achieve  an  optimal  survival  rate,  several  measures  are  implemented:    •  Irrigating the trees to improve survival rates.  Pruning  tall  trees  with  numerous  branches,  based  on  the  forestry  •  manager’s judgement.  •  Replanting trees according to the forest’s survival situation.  Weeding  as  necessary  to  minimize  the  impact  of  weeds  on  young  •  forests.  •  Setting  up  fences  and  a  forest  ranger  system  to  mitigate  human  impact on forest survival.  •  Constructing grass grids to prevent sand shifting and protect young  seedlings.  •  Inter-planting bushes and other vegetation among the trees.  •  Regularly conducting pest control and fire control operations.  Inventory work primarily involves random surveys to assess survival  •  rates, maintenance rates, tree height, basal diameter, and diameter  at breast height (DBH).  ree plantation was divided into stands on the basis of the crop age:  Year 1-3: Young Stand  Year 4-10: Middle Stand  Year 16-30: Mature Stand

The project created carbon 
sinks by afforestation of barren or degraded lands, which would continue to remain barren or 
degraded in the absence of the project activity, by direct planting native trees (China fir, Masson pine 
and Pinus yunnanensis) in planting areas of 1,370.96 ha.
The project not only sequester carbon through 
biomass growth but also provide native habitat for native species and provide employment for local 
workers in the adjoining forest management activities.

Life on land.summary: The evidence provided does not directly measure the indicator variable 'Trends in species diversity' as it lacks explicit quantitative data on the number of tree species, percentage of native species, and percentage of forest area with low tree species diversity. However, it does provide some qualitative information on the types of tree species present and the proportion of forest area occupied by different tree species. The evidence from the document '07-Tongliao_AR_GS3031_Final+Performance+Certification+Report_CC.pdf' mentions that the project area has been managed to enhance the biodiversity of native ecosystems, with a minimum of 10% of the project area identified and managed for this purpose (Page 77). Additionally, the document 'Tongliao_FVerR_Performance+Certification_New+Area_20210625.pdf' provides information on the proportion of forest area occupied by different tree species, with Poplar occupying approximately 48.3% and Pinus sylvestris occupying approximately 51.7% of the total area (Page 73).

Means of validation  DR, OSV: Conclusion | : T   D   V  T Based on the review of supporting documents and on-site inspection/i-xii/, VVB  confirms through reforestation with native or naturalised tree species: Pinus  sylvestris and Poplar spp.  he  primary  tree  species  in  the  area  are  poplar  and  Pinus  sylvestris,  with  poplar  covering  662.56  hectares  and  Pinus  sylvestris  occupying  708.44  hectares.  Additionally,  bushes  have  been  planted  to  stabilize  the  sands.  Fences  and  sand/grass  grids  are  also  utilized  to  control  desertification  and  reduce sand dune mobility.  uring the on-site inspection, VVB conducted interviews/i-xii/ with all personnel  from  Shanghai  Roots  &  Shoots,  the  Forest  Department,  and  Experts  of  Climate Bridge (Shanghai) Ltd., including the Monitoring Manager and staff,  and  confirmed  that  activities  such  as  planting  and  replanting,  weeding,  pruning,  intermediate  cutting,  pest  control,  and  sand  dune  stabilization  are  carried out for plantation establishment.  VB,  based  on  the  on-site  inspection  and  through  the  interviews  with  the  relevant stakeholder, confirms that the main focus is on ensuring the survival  of  the  trees.  To  achieve  an  optimal  survival  rate,  several  measures  are  implemented:    •  Irrigating the trees to improve survival rates.  Pruning  tall  trees  with  numerous  branches,  based  on  the  forestry  •  manager’s judgement.  •  Replanting trees according to the forest’s survival situation.  Weeding  as  necessary  to  minimize  the  impact  of  weeds  on  young  •  forests.  •  Setting  up  fences  and  a  forest  ranger  system  to  mitigate  human  impact on forest survival.  •  Constructing grass grids to prevent sand shifting and protect young  seedlings.  •  Inter-planting bushes and other vegetation among the trees.  •  Regularly conducting pest control and fire control operations.  Inventory work primarily involves random surveys to assess survival  •  rates, maintenance rates, tree height, basal diameter, and diameter  at breast height (DBH).  ree plantation was divided into stands on the basis of the crop age:  Year 1-3: Young Stand  Year 4-10: Middle Stand  Year 16-30: Mature Stand

Life on land.summary: The evidence from Tongliao_FVerR_Performance+Certification_New+Area_20210625.pdf, Page: 77, mentions that the project has successfully planted and managed 100% of the project area with poplar and Pinus sylvestris, enhancing the biodiversity of native ecosystems. However, this information does not directly measure the distribution and traits of native tree species in terrestrial ecosystems affected by invasive species. The evidence does not provide explicit quantitative data on the impact of invasive species on native tree species. Therefore, the evidence fails to satisfy the measurement requirement of the indicator.

SDG 8 
The project planted 1,370.96 hectares of native tree species on the sandy land, 
including pinus sylvestris and poplar.
In order to maintain the normal growth of these 
seedlings, the project owner hired forest rangers to manage.
According to the Electronic 
payment records of local payment system, a total of 11 forest rangers were employed.
Therefore, the estimated SDG 8 project outcome is significantly increased.

The planting area covers the whole 
project area except for two roads inside.
All the project sites were illegally logged before 1950s.
There is no natural renewal and 
reforestation before the project, all sites are degraded lands with some semi-fixed and fixed 
sand dunes.
The mainly planted tree species is Hybrid poplars for the project.
The implementation of the project would reduce the GHG emissions for 106,604 tCO2e in 30 
years, with an average annual GHG emission of 3,553 tCO2e.
